mission.title: Install and Configure MicroK8s
mission.description: MicroK8s is a lightweight, single-package Kubernetes distribution designed for simplicity and speed, ideal for development environments, IoT, and edge computing. It provides a fully conformant Kubernetes experience with minimal overhead, making it easy to set up and manage. Key trade-offs include limited scalability compared to full-fledged Kubernetes distributions and reliance on the Snap package manager.

steps.title: Install MicroK8s
steps.description:
This step installs MicroK8s using Snap, which is the recommended installation method for its simplicity and ease of use.

```bash

sudo snap install microk8s --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of MicroK8s.

steps.title: Add User to MicroK8s Group
steps.description:
To allow your user to run MicroK8s commands without sudo, you need to add your user to the microk8s group.

```bash

sudo usermod -a -G microk8s $USER

newgrp microk8s

```

> Replace $USER with your username if not using the current user.
> Apply the new group membership immediately.

steps.title: Verify MicroK8s Installation
steps.description:
Check that MicroK8s is running and verify the status of the cluster.

```bash

microk8s status --wait-ready

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Waits until MicroK8s is fully initialized.
> Lists the nodes in the cluster to confirm it's operational.

steps.title: Enable Add-ons
steps.description:
MicroK8s comes with several add-ons that can be enabled to extend its functionality.

```bash

microk8s enable dns

microk8s enable dashboard

```

> Enables the DNS add-on for service discovery.
> Enables the Kubernetes dashboard for UI management.

uninstall.title: Remove MicroK8s
uninstall.description:
This step will remove MicroK8s from your system. Be aware that this will delete all Kubernetes resources and configurations.

```bash

sudo snap remove microk8s

```

> This command will uninstall MicroK8s.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MicroK8s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and resources. The upgrade process will update MicroK8s to the latest version.

```bash

microk8s stop

sudo snap refresh microk8s

microk8s start

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Stops the MicroK8s service before upgrading.
> Upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version.
> Starts MicroK8s after the upgrade.
> Verifies that the nodes are operational after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'microk8s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s service may not be started.

**Fix:**
Run 'microk8s start' to start the service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'permission denied' when running microk8s commands
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** User is not part of the microk8s group.

**Fix:**
Add the user to the microk8s group using 'sudo usermod -a -G microk8s $USER'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:8080: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s is not running or kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure MicroK8s is running with 'microk8s status' and set up kubeconfig with 'microk8s kubectl config view --raw > $HOME/.kube/config'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no resources found' when checking services
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** No services have been deployed yet.

**Fix:**
Deploy a service using 'microk8s kubectl apply -f <your-service-file.yaml>'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features such as new API resources and improved scheduling capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs that are no longer recommended for use.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new API changes and update your manifests accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' when running 'microk8s kubectl get nodes', indicating that the nodes are ready and operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the resource requirements and has Snap installed.
